column5:
April 1, 2025

YouTube

An interesting Godardian experiment for Herzog to begin with. Not a huge reaction out of me, but it's cool how he contrasted images of a bodybuilder with shots of industrialized death and destruction to show how, no matter how strong, man is no match for modern technology. Pretty good for a debut short!

April 4, 2025

Internet Archive

The way it ended left me a tad confused, but everything until then was pretty fantastic actually. Great editing, performances, and a well-structured story about jealousy and feeling empty. Loved it.

Soderbergh explaining it: https://archive.org/details/winston-criterion-ld

column2: Popeye the Sailor Meets Sindbad the Sailor

column5:
September 23, 2025

In class

Not saying anything particularly new and a bit grating for how much it throws at you, but I respect how the animation is used and conceptually it’s pretty cool. Great sound design. Kinda like PlayTime lite, and interesting that my professor said that the best short films are plotless films with great vibes and just a brief glimpse into a world. Something to think about

October 5, 2025

For class (on a Google Drive link)

Pretty sick imagery and sound work and I can't believe a film student made this! My professor said this was made at DePaul and I can't quite wrap my head around that. Love that final cut a lot.

October 5, 2025

For class (through a Vimeo link)

This one was cool too. Feels like it's trying to be a bigger story than it actually is, and it's certainly well-directed with enough heart shining through that it would probably warrant being a feature, but as a short it's still an interesting watch at least, despite not really strongly gripping me at any point. I liked it though, this guy is really good at directing slower scenes that are captivating to watch, which definitely can't always be said for short films.

October 5, 2025

For class (Vimeo link)

Best DJRE film I've seen thus far. Feels like the most complete story for its runtime, the atmospheric sound design and cinematography are at their most immersive and most narratively significant here, the scene in the interrogation room was a stunner, and it left me with questions during its ending but I still felt emotionally satisfied based on images and feelings alone. Great stuff.

October 6, 2025

For class (through a Vimeo link)

Surprising ending that made me like this more!

column2: A Black Hole Near Kent County
column5:
October 7, 2025

For class (through a Vimeo link)

October 14, 2025

In class

Honestly?? Some kind of a miracle and unicorn. Actually really terrifying, an amazing portrayal of social anxiety, feeling too vulnerable and uncomfortable when you don’t fit in and just want to escape, feeling too weird to be loved, and the existential nature of how your social anxieties when you grow up are forever going to be a memory that you can’t do anything about and you have to live with that forever. The TVs at the start and end… wow. An analog capsule of pain and agitation.

December 6, 2025

AMC Naperville (after The Whole Bloody Affair)

December 27, 2025

YouTube

I live for this slice-of-life shit on such a small scale, and seeing Panahi shoot on an iPhone was so inspiring. Might be looking back at this for documentary editing and cinematography inspiration.

December 27, 2025

Vimeo

The central bit is actually very strong, I just wish it didn't just leave the same bit to be the overall message and wish it went a little deeper than the awkward comedy of it. She didn't even get to say her piece to her mom! Solid for what it is though, a student film that genuinely feels professionally made in terms of camera, sound, editing, directing, acting, etc. which I want nothing more than to achieve.
